steps.title: Use the no-solution shortcut
steps.content: A linear system has no solution when the two lines are parallel and distinct. Rewrite the given equation: $$30x=1800y-3600 \Rightarrow y=\frac{x}{60}+2$$ So the second line needs slope $\frac{1}{60}$ but a different $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Graph all choices in Desmos
steps.content: Graph the given line with each possible second line. Choice A, $\frac{1}{30}x=2y$, becomes $y=\frac{x}{60}$, which has the same slope as the given line but intercept $0$ instead of $2$. Those two lines are parallel and never intersect.

steps.title: Eliminate the distractors
steps.content: Choice B has slope $\frac{1}{1800}$, so it intersects the given line. Choice C has slope $\frac{1}{2}$, so it also intersects. Choice D is the same as the given line, which would give infinitely many solutions. Only choice A gives parallel distinct lines.
